summary: Enterprise Portal blank screen bug fix VERIFIED. Full login to dashboard flow working correctly. Backend APIs properly return session_token for localStorage backup, and accept X-Session-Token header as fallback to cookies.

issues: Console warning about chart width/height being -1, but charts render correctly. Non-blocking.

verified_features: POST /api/enterprise/auth/login returns session_token for localStorage backup
GET /api/enterprise/auth/me accepts X-Session-Token header as fallback to cookies
GET /api/enterprise/dashboard/stats returns all required KPI data with authenticated session
GET /api/enterprise/dashboard/charts returns trend data (phishing, sos, revenue, users)
Login page loads correctly with data-testid attributes for testing
Dashboard loads after login - no blank screen
Sidebar navigation visible with all menu items
KPI stat cards render with correct data
Employee name and role displayed in header (CEO ManoProtect / Super Admin)
SOS indicator shows pending count (1 SOS button visible)

frontend.enterprise_login_page_loads: PASSED - login form visible with data-testid selectors
frontend.dashboard_content_renders: PASSED - not blank, all sections visible
frontend.sidebar_navigation: PASSED - Panel Principal and all menu items visible
frontend.kpi_cards: PASSED - Empleados Activos, Clientes Totales, SOS Pendientes, etc. displayed
frontend.sos_indicator: PASSED - 1 SOS button visible with pending count

critical_code_review_comments: Fix is complete - session_token now stored in localStorage as backup
Backend properly accepts X-Session-Token header in get_current_employee function
Frontend EnterpriseLogin.js stores token in localStorage after successful login
Frontend EnterprisePortal.js checkAuth function uses stored token as fallback
Null check for employee state prevents blank screen before auth completes
seed_data_creation: No seed data needed - using existing CEO user
context_for_next_testing_agent: Enterprise portal blank screen bug is fixed. Login flow: POST /api/enterprise/auth/login -> returns session_token -> stored in localStorage -> used as X-Session-Token header fallback for /auth/me. Dashboard loads correctly with all KPIs and charts.
notes: Minor console warnings about chart dimensions (-1 width/height) appear but charts render correctly. WebSocket connection warning is non-blocking.
